# KoreCare

> KoreCare arranges world-class medical treatment in South Korea for members of US health insurers. The insurer covers the procedure; KoreCare manages everything around it — care-plan matching, flights and visa, airport pickup, a dedicated English interpreter and coordinator, hospital-adjacent recovery accommodation, and follow-up coordination with the patient's US physician.

## What KoreCare is

- A managed medical-travel service for US insurance members referred for treatment in Korea.
- Partners only with internationally accredited (e.g. JCI) tertiary hospitals and clinics in Korea.
- Covered programs are confirmed with the member's insurer after a short records review before any commitment.

## Specialties / Programs

- Oncology — proton therapy, robotic cancer surgery, targeted therapy.
- Orthopedics & Spine — minimally-invasive / endoscopic spine procedures.
- Cardiac Surgery — coronary bypass and hybrid cardiac programs.
- Full Health Screening — executive deep screening (whole-body MRI + PET-CT).

## Key pages

- Home / Programs: https://global.safedoc.io/
- Treatments: https://global.safedoc.io/programs
- How It Works: https://global.safedoc.io/how-it-works
- Reviews: https://global.safedoc.io/reviews
- FAQ: https://global.safedoc.io/faq
- Contact: https://global.safedoc.io/contact

## How it works (5 steps)

1. Care plan & match — a covered program is matched to the member's records.
2. Travel arranged — flights, visa, and airport pickup for the member and a companion.
3. In-Korea support — dedicated English interpreter and coordinator.
4. Recovery stay — hospital-adjacent accommodation booked.
5. US aftercare — follow-up coordinated with the member's home physician.

## Notes

- Languages: English (primary). Service audience: United States; treatment location: South Korea.
- Prices shown are estimates; final out-of-pocket is confirmed with the insurer after records review.
